445	  \[
446	    U \times U
447	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
448	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
449	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
450	  \]
451	  where $\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G$ is the group-variety inverse and
452	  $m \colon G \times G \to G$ is the group law. The map $\Phi$ is rational by
453	  composition: its domain is at least $U \times U \subseteq X \times X$, which is dense
454	  because $U$ is dense in $X$.
455	
456	  \emph{Sub-step 2: $\Phi$-defined at $(x, x)$ $\Leftrightarrow$ $\varphi$-defined at
457	  $x$.} If $\varphi$ is defined at $x$, the explicit formula $\Phi(x, x) = e$ (the
458	  identity of $G$) shows $\Phi$ is defined at $(x, x)$. Conversely, if $\Phi$ is
459	  defined at $(x, x)$, openness of $\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$ in $X \times X$ produces an
460	  open set $W \ni (x, x)$ on which $\Phi$ is defined; by projection (taking the slice
461	  $\{x\} \times W' \subseteq W$ for some open $W' \subseteq X$, possibly shrinking
462	  $W'$ so $\varphi$ is defined on $W'$ as well --- $W'$ need not contain $x$), the
463	  identity
464	  \[
465	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
466	    \qquad u \in W',
467	  \]
468	  exhibits a regular representative of $\varphi$ at $x$. Hence $x \in
469	  \mathrm{Dom}(\varphi)$ if and only if $(x, x) \in \mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$.
470	
471	  \emph{Sub-step 3: rephrase via pullback of local rings.} The rational map $\Phi$
472	  pulls $\mathcal O_{G, e}$ back to a $\bar k$-subalgebra of the function field
473	  $K(X \times X)$:
474	  \[
475	    \Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \;\longrightarrow\; K(X \times X).
476	  \]
477	  Because $\Phi$ sends $(x, x)$ to $e$ wherever it is defined, $\Phi$ is defined at
478	  $(x, x)$ if and only if $\Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subseteq \mathcal O_{X
479	  \times X, (x, x)}$ (i.e.\ every local function on $G$ near $e$ has no pole at
480	  $(x, x)$ after pullback).
481	
482	  \emph{Sub-step 4: pole locus is pure codimension $1$.} The variety $X \times X$ is
483	  nonsingular (smoothness is stable under products over $\bar k$), so the Weil-divisor
484	  apparatus of \cref{def:codim1_cycles} and the order map of
485	  \cref{def:order_at_point} apply on $X \times X$. For any nonzero rational function
486	  $f \in K(X \times X)^\ast$ decompose its divisor as
487	  \[
488	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty,
489	  \]
490	  with $\mathrm{div}(f)_0$ and $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty$ effective and
491	  $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = \mathrm{div}(f^{-1})_0$ (Milne's notation, matching the
492	  project's $\div(f) = \sum \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y$ of
493	  \cref{def:principal_divisor}: $\mathrm{div}(f)_0$ collects the prime divisors with
494	  $\ord_Y(f) > 0$ and $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty$ those with $\ord_Y(f) < 0$). The local
495	  ring at $(x, x)$ can be characterised as
496	  \[
497	    \mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}
498	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
499	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
500	  \]
501	  Suppose $f \in K(X \times X)^\ast$ is not in $\mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}$;
502	  equivalently, $(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty)$. The pole divisor
503	  $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty$ is a finite sum of prime divisors of $X \times X$ (i.e.\ a
504	  closed subset of pure codimension $1$ in $X \times X$). Its intersection with the
505	  diagonal $\Delta_X \subseteq X \times X$ is a closed subset of pure codimension $1$
506	  in $\Delta_X$ (this is Milne's reference to AG~9.2, the standard fact that a prime
507	  divisor of $X \times X$ either contains $\Delta_X$ or meets it in pure codimension
508	  $1$; on a nonsingular variety this is an instance of Krull's principal-ideal theorem
509	  applied to the local equation of $\Delta_X$ inside $X \times X$).
510	
511	  \emph{Conclusion.} Suppose $f \colon X \dashrightarrow G$ is not defined on all of
512	  $X$. Pick $x \in Z(f)$. By Sub-steps~2--3, there is some $f_0 \in
513	  \Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e})$ with $(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty)$.
514	  By Sub-step~4 the intersection $\mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty) \cap
515	  \Delta_X$ is pure codimension $1$ in $\Delta_X$, passing through $(x, x)$.
516	  Identifying $\Delta_X$ with $X$ via the diagonal isomorphism, the corresponding
517	  closed subset of $X$ has pure codimension $1$ and passes through $x$. By Sub-step~2,
518	  every point of this codim-$1$ subset is a point where $\Phi$ is undefined on the
519	  diagonal, hence where $\varphi$ is undefined. Thus $Z(\varphi)$ contains a pure
520	  codim-$1$ closed subset passing through $x$; since $x \in Z(\varphi)$ was arbitrary,
521	  every irreducible component of $Z(\varphi)$ has codimension exactly $1$, i.e.\
522	  $Z(\varphi)$ is of pure codimension $1$.
523	\end{proof}
524	
525	\begin{remark}
526	  \label{rmk:milne_lem33_charfree}
527	  \textbf{Characteristic-free.} The above proof uses only: (i) the group law on $G$
528	  and its inverse (formal smoothness of $G$ as a group object); (ii) the Weil-divisor
529	  apparatus on the nonsingular variety $X \times X$ (Hartshorne~II.6, or
530	  \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} in the project's notation); (iii) the
531	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
532	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on $X \times X$. None of these has a characteristic
533	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
534	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the
535	  lemma in this generality at \S I.3, p.~17.)
536	\end{remark}
537	
538	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
539	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
540	
541	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-$1$ ruling-out) goes
542	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
543	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
544	the extension fails at a prime divisor $W$ if and only if some affine coordinate of
545	$f$ around the image $\overline{f(\eta_W)}$ has a strictly negative order
546	$\ord_W(\cdot) < 0$. This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
547	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
548	
549	\begin{theorem}
550	
551	\leanok
552	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
553	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
554	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg}
555	  \uses{def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles, def:indeterminacy_locus,
556	        lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}
557	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation $v_Y$ associated to a
558	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}$ as the
559	  % subring of $K(X)$ on which $v_Y \geq 0$) (read from
560	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
561	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $Y$ is a prime divisor on $X$, let $\eta \in Y$ be its generic
562	  % point. Then the local ring $\mathcal O_{\eta, X}$ of $X$ at $\eta$ is a discrete
563	  % valuation ring with quotient field $K$, the function field of $X$. We call the
564	  % corresponding discrete valuation $v_Y$ the \emph{valuation of $Y$}."
565	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let $f \in K^\ast$ be any nonzero rational function on $X$.
566	  % Then $v_Y(f)$ is an integer. If it is positive, we say $f$ has a \emph{zero} along
567	  % $Y$, of that order; if it is negative, we say $f$ has a \emph{pole} along $Y$, of
568	  % order $-v_Y(f)$."
569	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation $v_Y$ and the order map).}
570	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$, let $Y$ be a variety over $\bar k$,
571	  let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ be a rational map, and let $W \subseteq X$ be a
572	  prime divisor with generic point $\eta_W \in W$ (so
573	  $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1$). Let $(U, \varphi_U)$ be any representative of
574	  $f$ with $U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset$. The following are equivalent:
575	  \begin{enumerate}
576	    \item $f$ is defined at $\eta_W$ (i.e.\ $\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$, equivalently
577	      $W \not\subseteq Z(f)$).
578	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood $V \subseteq Y$ of
579	      the generic image of $f$ near $W$, every regular function $g \in
580	      \mathcal O_Y(V)$ pulls back to a rational function $\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
581	      satisfying
582	      \[
583	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
584	      \]
585	  \end{enumerate}
586	
587	  Consequently: $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free
588	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
589	  $W \subseteq X$ and every affine open $V \subseteq Y$ as above, the pullback
590	  $\varphi_U^\ast(g)$ has $\ord_W \geq 0$ for all $g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)$.
591	\end{theorem}
592	
593	\begin{proof}
594	  \uses{def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles, lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr,
595	        thm:codim_one_extension}
596	  \leanok
597	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}$ is a
598	  DVR with fraction field $K(X)$ and uniformiser determined by $W$; by
599	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly $\ord_W$. The local ring at
600	  $\eta_W$ is therefore characterised inside $K(X)$ as the subring
601	  \[
602	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
603	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
604	  \]
605	  The rational map $f$ is defined at $\eta_W$ if and only if, for any affine open
606	  $V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y$ around the image $\overline{f(\eta_W)}$,
607	  the pullback $\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)$ lands in
608	  $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}$: this is the standard local characterisation of where a
609	  rational map is regular at a codim-$1$ point of a smooth variety (it is the
610	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
611	  equivalent to $\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0$ for every $g \in A$, which is
612	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~$\Leftrightarrow$~(2) is therefore established.
613	
614	  For the consequence, $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor $W$ is
615	  contained in $Z(f)$, which by the equivalence above is iff the $\ord_W \geq 0$
616	  condition holds for every prime divisor $W$.
617	\end{proof}
618	
619	\begin{remark}
620	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
621	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
622	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
623	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
624	  isolation, the abstract codim-$\geq 2$ statement
625	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
626	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
627	  $\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}$ API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
628	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-$0$ classifying-map
629	  arguments and in the symmetric-product Albanese chapter
630	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}.
631	\end{remark}
632	
633	\section{Lean encoding}
634	\label{sec:codim1_lean_encoding}
635	
636	The Lean target file is the new
637	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean}. The declarations to
638	introduce, in order:
639	
640	\begin{enumerate}
641	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} ---
642	    \cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}, a method on the Mathlib
643	    \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure. Implementation: \texttt{X.carrier
644	    \textbackslash{} f.domain} as a \texttt{Set X}, paired with an
645	    \texttt{IsClosed} witness (either bundled or as a separate
646	    \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus} lemma).
647	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} ---
648	    \cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}, a \texttt{Prop}-valued predicate. Encoding:
649	    \texttt{$\forall$ $\eta$ : X, Order.coheight $\eta$ = 1 $\to$ $\eta$ $\in$
650	    f.domain} (matching the \texttt{Order.coheight} idiom of
651	    \cref{def:prime_divisor}).
652	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} ---
653	    \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, a lemma (or instance) producing
654	    \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing (Scheme.LocalRing.at\ X $\eta$)} under the
655	    typeclass set \texttt{[Smooth ($\Spec \bar k$) X] [IsIntegral X]} and the
656	    hypothesis \texttt{Order.coheight $\eta$ = 1}.
657	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend\_of\_codimOneFree\_of\_smooth}
658	    --- \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}, the existence-of-extension theorem. Signature
659	    (informal): given a smooth variety $X$, a complete variety $Y$, and
660	    $f$~codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free, produce a unique regular morphism
661	    $\widetilde f \colon X \to Y$ extending $f$. Consumes
662	    \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} from
663	    \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} as a Mathlib-style import.
664	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy\_pure\_codim\_one\_into\_grpScheme}
665	    --- \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}. Signature (informal): given a smooth
666	    variety $X$, a group scheme $G$ over $\bar k$ that is a variety (smooth, finite
667	    type, separated), and $f \colon X \dashrightarrow G$ rational, conclude that
668	    $Z(f)$ is either empty or of pure codimension $1$ in $X$.
669	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend\_iff\_order\_nonneg}
670	    --- \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}, the Weil-divisor reformulation. Re-uses
671	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order} from
672	    \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} (the order map at a prime divisor).
673	\end{enumerate}
674	
675	\paragraph{Mathlib readiness audit.} At commit \texttt{b80f227} the prover should
676	verify:
677	\begin{itemize}
678	  \item \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain} is shipped at
679	    \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; its complement
680	    \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} is a one-line definition.
681	  \item The valuative criterion of properness in Mathlib form is
682	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.valuative\_criterion} (or analogous); the
683	    prover invokes this in Step~1 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof.
684	  \item Mathlib's smoothness predicate
685	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth} bundles the regular-local-rings hypothesis; the
686	    DVR-at-codim-1 lemma reduces to ``regular local of dim 1 = DVR''
687	    (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.isDiscreteValuationRing\_of\_dim\_one} or similar).
688	  \item Mathlib's product-of-smooth-is-smooth instance,
689	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.comp\_pullback} / equivalent, is needed in
690	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}'s Sub-step~4 to apply the Weil-divisor
691	    machinery to $X \times X$.
692	  \item The local-cohomology vanishing in Step~2 of
693	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof
694	    ($H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0$ at a
695	    depth-$\geq 2$ point) is a substantial Mathlib gap as of \texttt{b80f227}; the
696	    prover's options are (i) prove the depth-$\geq 2$ extension property as a local
697	    lemma using the \texttt{Module.depth} / Auslander--Buchsbaum machinery of
698	    \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}, or (ii) reformulate
699	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} in the weaker ``surface'' form
700	    (where $X$ has $\dim 2$, so codim-$\geq 2$ means closed points only and the
701	    extension is the classical Hartshorne~III.7.1 ``smooth-surface point removal''
702	    argument). For the consumer \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} only
703	    the surface case is needed in practice (the symmetric product $C^{(g)}$ is
704	    smooth, and the Abel--Jacobi map's intermediate-step indeterminacy lives on a
705	    smooth surface inside $C^{(g)} \times C^{(g)}$).
706	\end{itemize}
707	
708	No new core axiom is required. The proof of \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is
709	purely Weil-divisor manipulation on a smooth variety and is the cleanest part of
710	the chapter; the substance of the Lean engineering is in
711	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof, both halves of which rest on Mathlib API
712	gaps the prover must either fill in or work around.

714	\section{Out of scope}
715	\label{sec:codim1_out_of_scope}
716	
717	This chapter packages \emph{only} the two extension inputs and the Weil-divisor
718	reformulation needed for A.4.a. The following adjacent content is explicitly out of
719	scope:
720	\begin{itemize}
721	  \item \textbf{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular $\Rightarrow$
722	    Cohen--Macaulay corollary.} Proved in \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
723	    as \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}; this
724	    chapter only consumes them (in Step~2 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}; see
725	    \cref{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}).
726	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.2 itself.} The combination of
727	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} into the
728	    statement ``a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
729	    extends'' lives in \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} as
730	    \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}; this chapter only supplies the two inputs.
731	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.4 / Corollary~3.7 / Theorem~3.8 / Proposition~3.9 /
732	    Proposition~3.10.} The remainder of Milne~\S I.3 (the ``map between AVs is
733	    translated homomorphism'' family of results, and the
734	    $\mathbb A^1 \dashrightarrow A$ / unirational$\dashrightarrow A$ constancy
735	    statements) is downstream of \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} but is not
736	    needed by A.4 in the project's current routing: the genus-$0$ base case is
737	    handled via the proven Rigidity Lemma + $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut
738	    (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), and the positive-genus arm consumes only the bare
739	    Theorem~3.2 statement.
740	  \item \textbf{Weil-divisor degree on $X \times X$ for $X$ a curve.} The proof of
741	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} invokes the Weil-divisor apparatus on the
742	    smooth surface $X \times X$ but does \emph{not} need the degree map of
743	    \cref{def:divisor_degree} (which is curve-specific); only the codim-$1$
744	    decomposition $\div(f) = \div(f)_0 - \div(f)_\infty$ on a nonsingular surface is
745	    used. The bilinear degree formula on $X \times X$ for $X$ a curve (intersection
746	    numbers, Hartshorne~V.1) is out of scope.
747	  \item \textbf{Cartier divisors and the Cartier/Weil comparison.} On a smooth variety
748	    they coincide and the chapter uses Weil throughout; the Cartier formalism is not
749	    introduced.
750	  \item \textbf{Generalisation to non-algebraically-closed base fields.} The
751	    project's positive-genus arm runs over $\bar k$ throughout; descent of the
752	    extension to a non-algebraically-closed base, if ever needed, would require
753	    Galois-descent machinery and is not in scope.
754	  \item \textbf{Resolution of indeterminacy by blow-ups} (Hironaka-style, for higher
755	    indeterminacy codimensions on a smooth variety with a non-group-variety target):
756	    out of scope. The combination of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and
757	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} for $G$ a group variety is sharper than any
758	    blow-up-based approach because it produces an extension on the original $X$
759	    rather than on a blow-up; no blow-up apparatus is needed.
760	\end{itemize}

160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/

172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a Krull-dim-≤-1 stalk. -/
173	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
174	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk has Krull dimension at
175	  most one. (For a regular-in-codim-1 scheme the stalk is in fact a DVR; we
176	  only export the weaker Krull-dim bound here, sufficient to invoke
177	  `Ring.ordFrac`.) -/
178	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
179	
180	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
181	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
182	prime divisor `Y`. -/
183	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
184	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
185	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
186	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
187	
188	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
189	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
190	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
191	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.

203	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
204	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/

import Mathlib

-- The key chain: IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ finrank cotangentSpace = ringKrullDim R
-- We want to convert ringKrullDim = 1 (in WithBot ℕ∞) into finrank = 1 (in ℕ)
-- Let me check the form of IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace

#check @IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace
-- ↑(Module.finrank ...) = ringKrullDim R

-- So if hreg : IsRegularLocalRing R and hdim : ringKrullDim R = 1, then
-- ↑finrank = ringKrullDim R = 1 (in WithBot ℕ∞), so finrank ≤ 1 in ℕ.

example (R : Type) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    [IsRegularLocalRing R] (hdim : ringKrullDim R = 1) :
    Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) = 1 := by
  have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace R).mp inferInstance
  rw [hdim] at h
  exact_mod_cast h

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.WeilDivisor
8	
9	/-!
10	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
13	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
14	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
15	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
16	
17	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
18	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
19	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
20	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
21	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
22	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
23	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
24	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`extend_iff_order_nonneg`): a rational map
25	  extends at a prime divisor `W` iff every regular function on an affine
26	  neighbourhood of the image pulls back to a function of order `≥ 0` at `W`.
27	
28	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
29	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
30	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
31	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
32	
33	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
34	
35	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
36	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
39	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
40	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
41	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
42	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
43	
44	The 6 pinned declarations are:
45	
46	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
47	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
48	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
49	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
50	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
51	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
52	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
53	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
54	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
55	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
56	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
57	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg` (theorem, ~8 LOC) —
58	   Weil-divisor obstruction.
59	
60	## Note on type expressivity
61	
62	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
63	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
64	
65	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
66	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
67	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
68	  not `True`.
69	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
70	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
71	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
72	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
73	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
74	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
75	* `extend_iff_order_nonneg` asserts an iff between definedness at a prime
76	  divisor and the order-≥0 condition.
77	
78	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
79	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
80	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
81	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
82	
83	## Variety conventions
84	
85	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
86	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
87	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
88	
89	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
90	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
91	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
92	
93	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
94	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
95	
96	## References
97	
98	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` (~750 LOC,
99	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
100	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
101	-/
102	
103	set_option autoImplicit false
104	
105	universe u
106	
107	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
108	
109	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## §6. Weil-divisor reformulation of the codim-1 extension obstruction

The codim-1 extension obstruction of `thm:codim_one_extension` has a clean
reformulation in the project's Weil-divisor language: `f` extends at the
generic point of a prime divisor `W ⊆ X` iff every regular function on an
affine neighbourhood of the image pulls back to a function of order `≥ 0`
along `W` (no pole at `W`).

This is the form the consumer in
`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean` (A.4.d) needs when combining
`def:indeterminacy_locus`, `def:order_at_point`, and `def:principal_divisor`.

Blueprint pin: `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6 pp. 130–131). -/

/-- **Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction.**

For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor` a prime divisor
of `X` (encoded by its generic point `W.point` with codim-1 witness
`Order.coheight W.point = 1`), the following are equivalent:

1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e. `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.

The equivalence (1) ⇔ (2) is the Hartshorne II.6 "regular = no pole"
criterion for a rational map at a DVR-stalk codim-1 point: by
`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`, the stalk `O_{X,W.point}` is a DVR inside the
function field, and the standard valuation-ring characterisation
`O_{X,W.point} = {s ∈ K(X) | ord_W s ≥ 0} ∪ {0}` shows that the existence
of a representative defined at `W.point` is equivalent to all pullbacks
of regular functions on `Y` having order `≥ 0` at `W`. The Weil-divisor
identification feeds the existing `Scheme.RationalMap.order` API from
`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`.

Blueprint reference: `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne, *Algebraic
Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131; Stacks `02ME`).

iter-178+ body: by `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`, `O_{X,W.point}` is a DVR
inside the function field with valuation `Scheme.RationalMap.order W`; a
rational map is defined at `W.point` iff its pullback lands inside this
order-≥0 subring (the standard "regular = no pole" criterion). The forward
implication uses the canonical "partial map at W.point" extraction from
`Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`; the reverse implication invokes the
valuative criterion applied at the DVR `O_{X,W.point}`. -/
theorem extend_iff_order_nonneg
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]
    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.left.presheaf.stalk W.point)] :
    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩

/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point

A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
witnesses are constructed in this project).

### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)

The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement

> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,

which requires the pullback machinery
`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).

iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
the pullback machinery lands.

Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/

/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**

For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
are equivalent:

1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.

This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
construct partial-map representatives).

**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩

/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
stalk (Stacks `00PD`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
`ringKrullDim = 1` has `spanFinrank (maximalIdeal) = 1`, i.e.\ a principal
nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`). The full chain
`Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing` + `coheight z = 1 ⟹ ringKrullDim = 1` is a
substantive Mathlib gap (commit `b80f227` only ships the definition
`IsRegularLocalRing`, not the smooth-scheme bridge); we package the conclusion
locally as a single helper sorry. -/
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ :=
  sorry
input.new_string:
/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
stalk (Stacks `00PD`/`00TT`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
`ringKrullDim = 1` has principal nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`),
via the cotangent-space `finrank = 1` characterisation.

**Iter-179 Lane D body.** The Mathlib bridges
- `Algebra.Smooth k A → IsRegularLocalRing A` at every point (Stacks `00TT`
  Jacobian criterion side, scheme-level packaging), and
- `Order.coheight z = 1 → ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1`
  (the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge that
  `Scheme.RationalMap.order`'s `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` instance is gated on,
  see `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` L148-151)

do not ship in Mathlib at commit `b80f227`. We package both as a single
internal `sorry` (the `hreg_dim` hypothesis below) and discharge the
remaining principal-nonzero conclusion via Mathlib's
`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` +
`IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff` chain. The internal sorry
documents exactly the bridge needed: once the smooth-scheme regularity
and coheight-Krull-dim instances land in Mathlib (or a project upstream),
they discharge `hreg_dim` and the helper closes axiom-clean.

The previous body was a bare `sorry`; this Lane-D refactor preserves the
warning count at 1 (one sorry per declaration) while exposing the proof
skeleton for the next prover. -/
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
  -- The Mathlib-gap content, packaged as a single hypothesis: smoothness +
  -- codim-1-witness ⟹ the stalk is a regular local ring of Krull dimension 1.
  -- Stacks `00TT` (Jacobian criterion for smoothness ⟹ regular local) and
  -- the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge (Stacks `02IZ`/`005X`)
  -- together discharge this; neither half ships in Mathlib at b80f227.
  have hreg_dim : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ∧
                    ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := sorry
  obtain ⟨hreg, hdim⟩ := hreg_dim
  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
  have hfin :
      Module.finrank
        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
    rw [hdim] at h
    exact_mod_cast h
  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro hbot
    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
    rw [hdim] at h0
    exact one_ne_zero (by exact_mod_cast h0)
  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩

# Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean — iter-179 Lane D

## Lane summary

**Path D2 (rename + binder drop + docstring relax) — COMPLETED.**
**Helper body — PARTIAL (single internal sorry capturing the combined
Mathlib gap; sorry-count-preserving).**

`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CodimOneExtension` GREEN.
File warnings: 3 sorries (unchanged from iter-178 close).

## `extend_iff_order_nonneg` (was L391) — Path D2 RESOLVED

### Decision

Path D1 (tighten signature to use `Scheme.RationalMap.order`) requires
substantive new pullback machinery `RationalMap → function-field ring map`
that does not ship in Mathlib at `b80f227` and is not bounded by the
~30-LOC discharge cap the directive set. Path D2 is the auditor-178B-
recommended honest fallback and was selected.

### Changes

1. **Renamed** `extend_iff_order_nonneg` →
   `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` (now at L407 post-rename).
2. **Dropped** two unused typeclass binders that the substantive (Path D1)
   statement would have required but the actual 2-LOC proof does not use:
   `[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` and
   `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.left.presheaf.stalk W.point)]`.
3. **Updated docstrings**:
   - File header L24-32 — flagged that the substantive Weil-divisor
     reformulation is deferred to a future lemma carrying the pullback
     machinery; replaced the old `extend_iff_order_nonneg` bullet with
     the honest reshuffle bullet.
   - File header L57-61 — replaced the "Weil-divisor obstruction" pin
     description with the honest re-shuffle description.
   - File header L77-80 — corrected the "type expressivity" bullet.
   - Section L347-385 — rewrote section + lemma docstrings to describe
     the actual reshuffle content plus a clear "Status note" disclosing
     why Path D1 was deferred (Mathlib gap + scope) and naming the
     working title of the deferred substantive lemma
     (`extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`).
4. **Body unchanged** — the 2-LOC `mem_domain`-reshuffle proof is the
   maximal content the type now claims; the proof is exact.

### Cross-file impact (signature-mutating-lane audit)

`extend_iff_order_nonneg` was referenced in 2 places per `grep`:
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (own file) — all
  internal references updated by the rename.
- `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` — the `\lean{...}`
  cross-reference at `\label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}` now resolves
  to a renamed declaration; the blueprint will need a `\lean{...}` update
  by the review agent (cannot be done by the prover per role rules).

No `.lean` consumer file references this declaration externally.

### Auditor 178B follow-up

The auditor 178B finding — that `extend_iff_order_nonneg`'s docstring
claimed Hartshorne-II.6 valuative content the type did not encode — is
RESOLVED. The new name and docstrings honestly describe the proven
reshuffle content; the substantive content is openly disclosed as
deferred with the planned future-lemma name and gap reason.

## `smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot` (L222) — PARTIAL

### Status

iter-178 left this as `:= sorry` (bare). iter-179 Lane D replaced the
bare `sorry` with a structured proof exposing the closure path. The
warning count remains 1 (single internal `sorry` at L266 inside the
structured body) — net sorry delta = 0.

### Structure of the new body

The structured body decomposes the Mathlib-gap content into a single
named internal hypothesis `hreg_dim`:

```lean
have hreg_dim : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ∧
                  ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := sorry
```

and then proves the goal `IsPrincipal maximalIdeal ∧ maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`
axiom-clean from this hypothesis via the Mathlib chain:

1. `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`  ⇒
   `Module.finrank ResidueField CotangentSpace = ringKrullDim` (in
   `WithBot ℕ∞`). Combined with `ringKrullDim = 1` gives the cotangent
   `finrank = 1` (cast to `ℕ`).
2. `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff` ⇒
   `Submodule.IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal _)` from `finrank ≤ 1`.
3. `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` +
   `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField` ⇒ `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥` from
   `ringKrullDim = 1 ≠ 0`.

The Noetherian-stalk instance needed for the cotangent-space API is
threaded as a local `haveI` from `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`.

### Remaining Mathlib gap (the internal `sorry` at L266)

The single internal `sorry` discharges precisely:

> `Smooth X.hom + IsIntegral X.left + Order.coheight z = 1`
> ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing (stalk z) ∧ ringKrullDim (stalk z) = 1`

This combines TWO orthogonal Mathlib gaps at `b80f227`:

- **Gap (i)** — Smooth scheme stalk is regular local (the
  Stacks `00TT` Jacobian criterion at scheme-level packaging). Tried
  `exact?` on `[Field k] [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [Algebra k A]
  [Algebra.FormallySmooth k A] [IsNoetherianRing A] → IsRegularLocalRing A`
  in a standalone snippet; no Mathlib hit. Tried `exact?` on the
  scheme-level `[Smooth f : X ⟶ Y] [Smooth f : X ⟶ Spec (.of k)] →
  IsRegularLocalRing (X.presheaf.stalk x)`; no Mathlib hit either.

- **Gap (ii)** — Topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge:
  `Order.coheight z = 1 → ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = 1`. The
  ring `presheaf.stalk z` is the localisation at the prime corresponding
  to `z`; `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height` connects
  ringKrullDim of the local ring to the height of the prime, and
  `Order.coheight` on the topological space corresponds to the prime's
  height in `Spec`; the bridge is missing from Mathlib for general
  scheme stalks (cf. `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` L148-151 which
  flags the same gap for `Ring.KrullDimLE 1`).

The structured body documents both gaps in inline comments at the
declaration of `hreg_dim`. Once either gap closes upstream (likely as a
unified `Scheme.Smooth.isRegularLocalRing_stalk` or via Stacks `00TT`
scheme-level packaging), the helper closes axiom-clean.

### Why not split into a sub-helper

Helper budget for Lane D is 1; adding a named sub-helper for the
`hreg_dim` hypothesis would push budget to 2. The internal `sorry` here
*is* the helper-equivalent named gap, expressed inline with a
documentation comment — same content as a sub-helper would carry, no
budget overrun.

## File-level diagnostics

Pre-iter-179 (post-iter-178): 3 sorry warnings (L194, L288, L329).
Post-iter-179 Lane D: 3 sorry warnings (L222, L356, L397).
Net sorry delta: 0 (renames + structural insertions shifted line numbers).
No new axioms, no laundering. `lake build` GREEN.

## Lemmas / Mathlib lemmas confirmed usable this iter

- `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs`) — usable as `.mp` + cast.
- `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent`) — usable as `.mp` from
  `finrank ≤ 1`.
- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Basic`) — `.mpr` from `maximalIdeal = ⊥`.
- `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField` — closes the field/dim-0
  contradiction.
- `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian` — bridges
  `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]` → `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left` over a
  Noetherian base (Spec of a field is automatic).

## Negative findings

- No Mathlib lemma directly bridging `Algebra.Smooth k A` (or any
  scheme-level `Smooth`) to `IsRegularLocalRing` at `b80f227`.
- No Mathlib lemma directly bridging `Order.coheight z` on
  `X.presheaf.stalk z`'s carrier to `ringKrullDim`.
- The directive's path "via `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`
  plus a `coheight ≥ 1` ⟹ `dim ≥ 1` chain" requires gap (ii) to land;
  Lane D produced the structural chain but cannot discharge gap (ii)
  in-iter.

## Next-step recommendations for iter-180 Lane D follow-up

1. **Gap (i) closure** — file a Mathlib-upstream / project-local lemma
   `Scheme.IsSmooth.isRegularLocalRing_stalk` or equivalent:

   ```
   theorem Scheme.IsSmooth.isRegularLocalRing_stalk
     {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [hf : Smooth f] [IsField (Γ(Y, ⊤))]
     (x : X) : IsRegularLocalRing (X.presheaf.stalk x)
   ```

   Stacks tag `00TT` (Jacobian criterion) at the algebraic level;
   tag `01V3` for the geometric packaging. Per the auto-injected catalog,
   this is exactly the kind of foundational Mathlib gap a `mathlib-analogist`
   consult could break (`smooth-stalk-regular` slug).
2. **Gap (ii) closure** — file the coheight-to-ringKrullDim bridge:

   ```
   theorem Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_of_coheight (X : Scheme.{u})
     [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (z : X) :
     ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = Order.coheight z
   ```

   via `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height` +
   `PrimeSpectrum.coheight_eq_height` (the latter is the lemma whose
   existence we'd verify). Project-local would also be fine since
   `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` already needs the
   `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` instance from the same data.
3. Either gap closure on its own is insufficient; both must land for
   the helper to close. Until both land, the structured body is the
   max content extractable.

## Unrelated declarations (Lane D non-targets, untouched per directive)

- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (L356) — still `sorry`.
  Lane D directive forbid touching.
- `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (L397) — still `sorry`.
  Lane D directive forbid touching.

## Blueprint feedback for the review agent

The blueprint file
`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`'s
`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg}`
cross-reference at `\label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}` (L554) now
resolves to a renamed declaration. Two acceptable review-agent
corrections:

- **Option A (recommended)**: change the `\lean{...}` to the new name
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
  AND demote the blueprint statement of `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`
  to match the actual proven content (the lightweight reshuffle), with
  a `% NOTE: substantive Hartshorne-II.6 reformulation deferred to a
  future lemma; this pin tracks the iter-179 Lane D Path D2 honest
  fallback.` annotation explaining the demotion.
- **Option B (heavier)**: keep the blueprint statement as the
  substantive Hartshorne content and remove the `\lean{...}` pin until
  the substantive lemma lands, replacing the `\leanok` marker with
  `% NOTE: not yet formalised; iter-179 Lane D Path D2 deferred the
  substantive content to a future lemma `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`.`

Option A is recommended because it keeps the blueprint+Lean in sync and
honestly documents the demotion; Option B is more bookkeeping but
preserves the substantive blueprint statement.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

8	/-!
9	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
10	variety extends (A.4.c)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
14	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
15	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
16	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
17	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
18	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
19	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
20	
21	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
22	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
23	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
24	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
25	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
26	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
27	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
28	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
29	defined.
30	
31	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
32	
33	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
34	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
35	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
36	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
37	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
38	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
39	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
40	statements.
41	
42	The 1 pinned declaration is:
43	
44	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
45	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
46	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
47	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
48	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
49	
50	## Note on type expressivity
51	
52	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
53	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
54	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
55	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
56	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
57	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
58	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
59	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
60	
61	## Abelian-variety conventions
62	
63	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
64	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
65	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
66	
67	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
68	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
69	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
70	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
71	
72	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
73	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
74	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
75	
76	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
77	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
78	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
79	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
80	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
81	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
82	
83	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
84	the call site.
85	
86	## References
87	
88	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
89	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
90	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
91	-/

101	namespace Scheme
