65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec
161	
162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211

280	/-! ## §5. Base change
281	
282	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
283	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
284	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
285	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
286	
287	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
288	lemma-spec-base-change). -/

291	
292	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
293	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
294	of `T`-schemes
295	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
296	
297	The pullback `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is bound by the existential; iter-174+
298	will refine to a named `pullbackQcoh g 𝒜` operation once `QcohAlgebra` is

312	  -- algebra to `T` along `g` (`Coequifibered` is stable under base change

12	## Question
13	
14	What is the right Mathlib-aligned shape for the body of
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme`
16	and its `structureMorphism`?
17	
18	The iter-176 "closure" set `RelativeSpec 𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`,
19	silently discarding `𝒜`. The lean-auditor iter-177 marked both
20	declarations CRITICAL "weakened-wrong" and the progress-critic flagged
21	Route 2 CHURNING with the corrective "Mathlib-analogist consult on
22	RelativeSpec encoding BEFORE another body fill". Four candidate
23	encodings were proposed: (a) `QcohAlgebra` carrier + `Scheme.GlueData`;
24	(b) `Spec` of a relative graded ring; (c) functor-of-points; (d) some
25	other Mathlib-aligned shape. The first three are wrong or expensive;
26	the right answer is (d) — Mathlib already ships the construction.

53	### The smoking gun
54	
55	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:240-255` carries
56	the section header
57	
58	```
59	## "Quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras"
60	
61	A presheaf `F` of rings on `X.AffineZariskiSite` with a structural
62	morphism `α : 𝒪ₓ ⟶ F` is said to be `Coequifibered` if
63	`F(D(f)) = F(U)[1/f]` for every open `U` and any section `f : Γ(X, U)`.
64	
65	Under this condition we can construct a family of gluing data (See
66	`relativeGluingData`) and glue `F` into a scheme over `X` via
67	`(relativeGluingData _).glued`.
68	[...] This is closely related to the notion of quasi-coherent
69	`𝒪ₓ`-algebras, and we shall link them together once the theory of
70	quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras are developed.
71	```
72	
73	Mathlib has packaged exactly the Stacks 01LL/01LM/01LP construction —
74	they just haven't *named* it `RelativeSpec` because the qcoh-algebra
75	theory linking the input shape to "QcohAlgebra" hasn't landed yet. The
76	already-shipped consumer `Hom.normalization`
77	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`) shows the exact

80	```lean
81	def normalizationGlueData := relativeGluingData f.coequifibered_normalizationDiagramMap
82	def normalization : Scheme := f.normalizationGlueData.glued
83	```

85	This is the canonical relative-Spec template the project should

88	## Decisions identified
89	
90	### Decision 1: choice of body for `RelativeSpec`
91	
92	- **Mathlib idiom**: `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData H).glued` where
93	  `H : (affineUnit 𝒜).Coequifibered` is the qcoh predicate after
94	  projecting `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra` to the (presheaf-on-`AffineZariskiSite`,
95	  natural-transformation) form Mathlib accepts. Cite:
96	  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293-299` +
97	  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102` (`.glued`). Why
98	  Mathlib chose it: the affine-cover construction (Stacks 01LM/01LP)
99	  is exactly a `Cover.RelativeGluingData` over the canonical directed
100	  affine open cover `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X`, with
101	  fibres `Spec(𝒜(U))` and transition isomorphisms induced by
102	  `IsLocalization.Away`. The locally-directed gluing of
103	  `RelativeGluing.lean` is the cleaner colimit-shaped restatement of
104	  the classical `Scheme.GlueData` (avoids the cocycle bookkeeping).
105	
106	- **Project's current path**: `RelativeSpec 𝒜 := X` (the base scheme
107	  itself). The `_𝒜` argument is silently discarded.
108	
109	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong. The carrier is wrong — it does not
110	  depend on `𝒜` at all, so every downstream theorem that quantifies
111	  over the relative spectrum is trivialized.
112	
113	- **Cost of divergence**: total. Auditor verdict iter-177 stands —
114	  Picard, line-bundle moduli, and the rest of the positive-genus arm
115	  cannot build a substantive `PicardFunctor` against a `RelativeSpec`
116	  that ignores its argument. Refactor obligation, not stylistic
117	  preference.
118	
119	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (critical, must-fix-this-iter).
120	
121	### Decision 2: shape of `QcohAlgebra` carrier (does it currently support the Mathlib construction?)
122	
123	- **Mathlib idiom**: the Mathlib `relativeGluingData` input is a
124	  *triple* `(F, α, H)`:
125	  1. `F : X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`,
126	  2. `α : (toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ F` (the algebra unit
127	     restricted to the affine Zariski site),
128	  3. `H : α.Coequifibered` (the qcoh predicate, equivalent to "every
129	     restriction-to-basic-open is `IsLocalization.Away`").
130	
131	  The project's `QcohAlgebra` (`RelativeSpec.lean:131-136`) currently
132	  packages `(sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X, unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf)` —
133	  exactly the data needed for parts (1) + (2) once restricted via
134	  `(toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ -`. **Part (3), the `Coequifibered`
135	  predicate, is MISSING** — this is the quasi-coherence overlay that
136	  `analogies/qcohalgebra-structure.md` flagged as iter-175+ work.
137	
138	  However the upstream-gap concern in that analogy was specifically
139	  about `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which would need the
140	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure). **`NatTrans.Coequifibered` is a
141	  weaker, equation-shaped predicate that DOES NOT need sheafified-tensor
142	  machinery** — it just asserts each restriction-to-basic-open is an
143	  `IsLocalization.Away`. This is provable directly from a sheaf-of-rings
144	  structure (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:266`
145	  is the equivalence with `IsLocalization.Away`).
146	
147	- **Project's current path**: Encoding I without the `coequifibered`
148	  field. The `iter-175+` plan in `RelativeSpec.lean:122-129` mentions
149	  *"add a third field `isQcoh : Prop`"* but the file has not yet been
150	  updated.
151	
152	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The carrier IS upgradable to support
153	  the Mathlib construction with a single new field; the present
154	  carrier alone is not.
155	
156	- **Cost of divergence**: one missing structure field. Until added,
157	  `relativeGluingData` cannot fire and the body of `RelativeSpec`
158	  cannot be the Mathlib value. ~10 LOC fix.
159	
160	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — add the `Coequifibered` field
161	  (in the `IsLocalization.Away` equivalent form via
162	  `coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway` for readability).
163	
164	### Decision 3: choice of body for `structureMorphism`
165	
166	- **Mathlib idiom**: `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData H).toBase`.
167	  Cite: `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`. The
168	  structure morphism is constructed as the colimit descent of the
169	  natural transformation `Xᵢ → Uᵢ` along the directed affine cover.
170	
171	- **Project's current path**: `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`. Wrong:
172	  identity has nothing to do with the algebra unit, and downstream
173	  theorems mentioning `pullback g (structureMorphism _𝒜)` collapse to
174	  `pullback g (𝟙 X) ≅ T`, which is the *wrong* substantive object.
175	
176	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (same as Decision 1).
177	
178	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (critical, must-fix-this-iter).
179	
180	### Decision 4: shape of the four downstream theorems
181	
182	- **Mathlib idiom**: each one is a *direct consequence* of the
183	  Mathlib-provided lemmas on `RelativeGluingData`:
184	
185	  - `UniversalProperty` (currently `IsAffineHom`): use
186	    `RelativeGluingData.cover` + the fact that each restriction is
187	    `Spec(F U) → U`, which is affine; then the
188	    `HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top` criterion
189	    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Affine.lean:146`) closes
190	    affineness. Once the project elevates `UniversalProperty` to a
191	    `Functor.RepresentableBy` statement, the building block is
192	    `RelativeGluingData.isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase`
193	    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:172`), which
194	    matches the Yoneda statement of Stacks 01LQ on each affine.
195	
196	  - `affine_base_iff` (Spec R case): when `X = Spec R`,
197	    `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite` has a top element `⊤ = X` and the
198	    glued scheme reduces to `Spec(F ⊤) = Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The
199	    `isColimit_cocone` lemma at
200	    `SmallAffineZariski.lean:343` is the affine-base instance of
201	    exactly this argument (with `α = 𝟙`); the project's
202	    affine-base reduction is the same proof with a non-trivial `F`.
203	
204	  - `base_change`: pullback the qcoh algebra to `T` (via the
205	    sheaf pullback adjunction `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:151`
206	    upgraded to sheaves of CommRings), use that `IsLocalization.Away`
207	    is stable under base change of rings, conclude the resulting
208	    natural transformation is again `Coequifibered`, then use Mathlib's
209	    universal property of the gluing.
210	
211	  - `functor` (object-level): `fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`
212	    — already correct in shape.
213	
214	- **Project's current path**: each of the three theorems hides behind
215	  the trivial body. After the carrier swap, each theorem is provable
216	  by referencing the Mathlib lemma.
217	
218	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — replace the trivializing
219	  proofs with the Mathlib-builder-based proofs. (For `UniversalProperty`
220	  the iter-178+ refinement to `Functor.RepresentableBy` is unblocked
221	  by the carrier swap because the per-affine Yoneda fact is now
222	  available as a Mathlib lemma.)
223	
224	## Recommendation
225	
226	**Adopt Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` as the carrier verbatim.**
227	This is not a refactor; it is a *replacement*. The placeholder body
228	`RelativeSpec := X` is destroyed and the value is given by the
229	Mathlib builder.
230	
231	Concrete refactor for `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean`:
232	
233	```lean
234	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Sites.SmallAffineZariski
235	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RelativeGluing
236	
237	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
238	
239	/-- A quasi-coherent sheaf of O_X-algebras: sheaf of CommRings, an O_X-algebra
240	unit, and the quasi-coherence overlay in Stacks-01LL form. -/
241	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
242	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
243	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
244	  /-- Stacks 01LL: on every affine open U, restriction to a basic open
245	  D(f) ⊆ U is the localization-away-from-f. -/
246	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered (X := X.AffineZariskiSite)
247	    (F := (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf)
248	    (G := (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ sheaf.val)
249	    (whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.val)
250	
251	namespace QcohAlgebra
252	
253	/-- The affine restriction of a qcoh algebra: its sheaf restricted to
254	the affine Zariski site, viewed as a presheaf to CommRingCat. -/
255	abbrev affinePresheaf (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
256	    X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u} :=
257	  (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ 𝒜.sheaf.val
258	
259	/-- The affine restriction of the unit. -/
260	abbrev affineUnit (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
261	    (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ 𝒜.affinePresheaf :=
262	  whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op 𝒜.unit.val
263	
264	end QcohAlgebra
265	
266	/-- The relative spectrum scheme. Built from Mathlib's
267	`AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`. -/
268	noncomputable def RelativeSpec (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
269	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
270	
271	/-- The structure morphism Spec_X(𝒜) → X. -/
272	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
273	    X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
274	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
275	```
276	
277	Each downstream theorem follows from the corresponding Mathlib lemma:
278	
279	- `UniversalProperty` (affine form): use the open cover
280	  `RelativeGluingData.cover`. Each fiber is `Spec(F U)` (affine by
281	  `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`), each restriction is
282	  `Spec(α.app (op U))` (an `IsAffineHom` between affines). Combine via
283	  `HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Affine.lean:146`).
284	
285	- `affine_base_iff`: on `Spec R`, `X.AffineZariskiSite` has a top
286	  element. The Mathlib `isColimitCocone` proof in
287	  `SmallAffineZariski.lean:343-366` shows the structure: the gluing
288	  collapses to `Spec(F ⊤) = Spec(𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op ⊤))`. Adapt to
289	  non-trivial `F`.
290	
291	- `base_change`: pullback of qcoh algebras (via `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackContinuous.lean` upgraded
292	  to CommRings) preserves `Coequifibered` (because `IsLocalization.Away`
293	  is preserved by ring base change — `Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/BaseChange.lean`).
294	  Then the universal property of the gluing gives the iso
295	  `T ×_X RelativeSpec(𝒜) ≅ RelativeSpec(g^* 𝒜)`.
296	
297	- `functor`: keep as-is, body is now substantive because
298	  `structureMorphism` is.
299	
300	## Pivot plan (iter-178 → iter-179+)
301	
302	**Block A — carrier upgrade (~25 LOC, iter-178 if a body-skeleton lane
303	can land it, else iter-179)**:
304	1. Add `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Sites.SmallAffineZariski` + `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RelativeGluing` imports (already brought in via `import Mathlib`).
305	2. Add `affinePresheaf` and `affineUnit` helpers (~10 LOC).
306	3. Add `coequifibered` field to `QcohAlgebra` (~5 LOC) — definition uses Mathlib's `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, NO new project axioms.
307	4. Replace `RelativeSpec` body with `(relativeGluingData _).glued` (~3 LOC).
308	5. Replace `structureMorphism` body with `.toBase` (~3 LOC).
309	
310	**Block B — downstream theorem rewrites (~60 LOC, iter-179)**:
311	6. `UniversalProperty (affine form)`: ~15 LOC via `HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top`.
312	7. `affine_base_iff`: ~25 LOC adapting `SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone`.
313	8. `base_change`: ~30 LOC using `Coequifibered` stability under pullback.
314	
315	**Block C — upgrade `UniversalProperty` to `Functor.RepresentableBy` (~40 LOC, iter-180+)**:
316	9. Build the Yoneda functor (the `O_X`-algebra Hom functor on schemes-over-`X`).
317	10. Use `RelativeGluingData.isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase` to prove the Yoneda
318	    bijection on the affine cover.
319	11. Glue via Mathlib's locally-directed cover hom-extension.
320	
321	## LOC estimate
322	
323	- **Minimum substantive landing** (Blocks A + B, removing the
324	  placeholder for good): **~85 LOC** net (about half of the current
325	  file is the existing structure header + docstrings, which mostly
326	  carry over).
327	- **Full alignment including representability** (A + B + C): **~125 LOC**.
328	
329	This is *materially smaller* than the 680-LOC estimate from
330	`analogies/qcohalgebra-structure.md` because Mathlib has already done
331	the gluing — the project does not need to invoke `Scheme.GlueData`
332	explicitly, nor build a sheafified-tensor monoidal structure.
333	
334	## Caveat on the `Coequifibered` field
335	
336	The `Coequifibered` predicate references natural transformations on
337	`X.AffineZariskiSite`, not on all of `X.Opens`. This is *strictly weaker*
338	than the full sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
339	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`. For a sheaf of CommRings on `X.Opens`,
340	the two are equivalent under the dense-subsite equivalence
341	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`
342	(`SmallAffineZariski.lean:203`), so no information is lost. The project
343	should `Coequifibered`-ize because that is the Mathlib idiom for the
344	input to `relativeGluingData`; the sheaf-of-modules predicate remains
345	the *long-term* form (Encoding II) but is not on the critical path
346	since `Coequifibered` already produces the correct geometric object.
347	
348	## Hard NO
349	
350	- **(a) `QcohAlgebra` carrier + `Scheme.GlueData`**: would duplicate
351	  Mathlib's `Cover.RelativeGluingData` (which is the colimit-shaped,
352	  locally-directed restatement of `Scheme.GlueData` *specifically* for
353	  this case). Reject — parallel API.
354	
355	- **(b) `Spec` of a relative graded ring**: only applies to *graded*
356	  algebras (`Proj`-construction, Stacks 01M3). The project needs the
357	  general `Spec_X` case for ungraded `𝒜`. Reject — narrower than the
358	  blueprint requires.
359	
360	- **(c) Functor-of-points**: the *output* shape (`UniversalProperty`)
361	  is functor-of-points. But the *carrier* should be a concrete scheme,
362	  not a representable functor's section, because every other piece of
363	  the file (open cover, base change, affine-base reduction) wants a
364	  concrete scheme. Reject as carrier; use as the *type* of the
365	  upgraded `UniversalProperty`.

1	\chapter{Relative Spec}
2	\label{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-172). This chapter is the foundational A.1.a sub-row of
6	% Route A (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% relative-spectrum functor \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}
8	% \to \mathrm{Sch}/X used by the relative Picard functor on a product C \times_k T.
9	% Mathlib has the absolute Spec functor and affine gluing (Scheme.GlueData,
10	% AffineScheme.glueOpens) but no general RelativeSpec construction; this chapter is
11	% the prover-ready specification for the new file AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
12	% which iter-173 (file-skeleton lane) will scaffold.
13	
14	\section{Setup and motivation}
15	\label{sec:relspec_setup}
16	
17	The Route~A construction of $J = \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as a $g$-dimensional abelian variety is
18	organised around the relative Picard functor
19	$\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)$
20	on the category of $k$-schemes. Representing $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ requires the line-bundle
21	pullback construction on a relative curve $C \times_k T \to T$, which in turn rests on a
22	\emph{relative scheme} construction on a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras
23	$\mathcal{A}$, the \emph{relative spectrum}
24	$\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$. This chapter packages the construction
25	of $\underline{\Spec}_X(-)$ and its three core properties --- universal property, affine
26	base, base change --- as the foundational A.1.a building block. The relative Picard
27	functor itself (A.1.c), the line-bundle pullback functor on a product (A.1.b), and the
28	identity-component / dimension theory of $J$ (A.3) live in separate sibling chapters
29	(\S~``Out of scope'' below).

32	% spectrum as a functor'' (tags 01LL, 01LO, 01LQ, 01LR, 01LS, available locally at
33	% references/stacks-constructions.tex L309-L720). The Hartshorne reference is
34	% [Hartshorne], II Exercise 5.17 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf).

36	\section{Quasi-coherent sheaves of algebras}
37	\label{sec:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
38	
39	\begin{definition}\leanok
40	[Quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra]
41	  \label{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
42	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}
43	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section 01LL ``Relative spectrum via glueing'',
44	  % Situation ``situation-relative-spec''
45	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L312-L318).
46	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
47	  % "Here $S$ is a scheme, and $\mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent
48	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra. This means that $\mathcal{A}$ is a
49	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras which is quasi-coherent as an
50	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-module."
51	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LL (situation-relative-spec).}
52	  Let $X$ be a scheme. A \emph{quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras} is a
53	  sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras (i.e. an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
54	  equipped with $\mathcal{O}_X$-linear multiplication and unit morphisms satisfying
55	  associativity, commutativity, and the unit law as sheaf morphisms) whose underlying
56	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is quasi-coherent. We write $\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)$ for the
57	  category of quasi-coherent sheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras and
58	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra morphisms.
59	\end{definition}

72	% adds the overlay as a 3rd field WITHOUT renaming the existing two — no

77	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
78	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}
79	
80	The construction is given affine-locally: on every affine open $U = \Spec R \subseteq X$ one
81	sets $\pi^{-1}(U) := \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ regarded as an $R$-algebra, and the local pieces
82	glue compatibly because $\mathcal{A}$ is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
83	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for an inclusion
84	$V = \Spec S \subseteq U$ gives an open immersion of the corresponding Specs).
85	
86	\begin{theorem}\leanok
87	[Relative spectrum exists]
88	  \label{thm:relative_spec_exists}
89	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}
90	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
91	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec)
92	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L381-L405).
93	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
94	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
95	  %  There exists a morphism of schemes
96	  %  $$
97	  %  \pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow S
98	  %  $$
99	  %  with the following properties:
100	  %  \begin{enumerate}
101	  %  \item for every affine open $U \subset S$ there exists an isomorphism
102	  %  $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \to \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$, and
103	  %  \item for $U \subset U' \subset S$ affine open the composition
104	  %  $$
105	  %  \xymatrix{
106	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \ar[r]^{i_U^{-1}} &
107	  %  \pi^{-1}(U) \ar[rr]^{inclusion} & &
108	  %  \pi^{-1}(U') \ar[r]^{i_{U'}} &
109	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))
110	  %  }
111	  %  $$
112	  %  is the open immersion of Lemma \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion} above.
113	  %  \end{enumerate}
114	  %  Moreover, $\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})$
115	  %  is unique up to unique isomorphism over $S$."
116	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec);
117	  cf.\ [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(a).}
118	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra
119	  (Definition~\ref{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}). There exists a scheme
120	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with an affine morphism
121	  $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$ such that
122	  \begin{enumerate}
123	    \item for every affine open $U \subseteq X$, there is an isomorphism
124	      $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$;
125	    \item for $U \subseteq U' \subseteq X$ both affine open, the composite
126	      $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \xrightarrow{i_U^{-1}} \pi^{-1}(U) \hookrightarrow
127	      \pi^{-1}(U') \xrightarrow{i_{U'}} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$
128	      coincides with the open immersion induced by the restriction
129	      $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$.
130	  \end{enumerate}
131	  The pair $(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}), \pi)$ is unique up to unique isomorphism
132	  over $X$.
133	\end{theorem}
134	
135	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Follows immediately from
136	% Lemmas \ref{lemma-relative-glueing},
137	% \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion}, and
138	% \ref{lemma-transitive-spec}.
139	% Uniqueness is stated in the last sentence of
140	% Lemma \ref{lemma-relative-glueing}."
141	\begin{proof}
142	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
143	  \leanok
144	  By the open-cover gluing principle for schemes (Mathlib
145	  \texttt{Scheme.GlueData} / \texttt{AffineScheme.glueOpens}) it suffices to specify
146	  affine local pieces $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ for each affine open $U \subseteq X$ and
147	  open-immersion transition data
148	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \hookrightarrow \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$ for $U \subseteq U'$
149	  satisfying the cocycle condition. The transition morphisms come from the ring maps
150	  $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$; quasi-coherence yields the cartesian square
151	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) = U \times_{U'} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$, so each transition is an
152	  open immersion. Transitivity (Stacks lemma-transitive-spec) gives the cocycle condition.
153	  Uniqueness of the glued scheme is the standard property of the gluing functor.
154	\end{proof}

156	\begin{theorem}\leanok
157	[Universal property of the relative spectrum]
158	  \label{thm:relative_spec_univ}
159	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty}
160	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists}
161	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
162	  % structural consequence ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than
163	  % the natural-bijection statement pinned by this prose. Iter-174+ must
164	  % upgrade the Lean type to a ``CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy''
165	  % witness (or equivalent) before any consumer file relies on this theorem.
166	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ continuation: Section ``Relative spectrum as
167	  % a functor'', functor F and Lemma lemma-spec
168	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L427-L466 + L547-L551).
169	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
170	  % "For any $f : T \to S$ the pullback
171	  %  $f^*\mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebras.
172	  %  We are going to consider pairs $(f : T \to S, \varphi)$ where
173	  %  $f$ is a morphism of schemes and $\varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$
174	  %  is a morphism of $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebras. Note that this is the
175	  %  same as giving a $f^{-1}\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra homomorphism
176	  %  $\varphi : f^{-1}\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$, see
177	  %  Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjointness-tensor-restrict}.
178	  %  This is also the same as giving an $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map
179	  %  $\varphi : \mathcal{A} \to f_*\mathcal{O}_T$, see
180	  %  Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-push-pull-modules}.
181	  %  We will use all three ways of thinking about $\varphi$,
182	  %  without further mention. [...]
183	  %  In this way we have defined a functor
184	  %  \begin{eqnarray}
185	  %  \label{equation-spec}
186	  %  F : \Sch^{opp} & \longrightarrow & \textit{Sets} \\
187	  %  T & \longmapsto & F(T) = \{\text{pairs }(f, \varphi) \text{ as above}\}
188	  %  \nonumber
189	  %  \end{eqnarray}
190	  %  [...]
191	  %  The functor $F$ is representable by a scheme."
192	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-spec + section-spec
193	  functor description); [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
194	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra.
195	  Then $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents the functor
196	  $F : \Sch^{op} \to \mathrm{Set}$,
197	  $T \mapsto F(T) := \{(f, \varphi) : f : T \to X \text{ a morphism},\;
198	  \varphi : f^* \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T \text{ an } \mathcal{O}_T\text{-algebra map}\}.$
199	  Equivalently, for any $X$-scheme $g : T \to X$,
200	  there is a natural bijection
201	  \[
202	    \Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \;\;\cong\;\;
203	    \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_* \mathcal{O}_T)
204	  \]
205	  (using the adjunction between $g_*$ and $g^*$ to convert between
206	  $\mathcal{A} \to g_*\mathcal{O}_T$ and $g^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$).
207	  The bijection is natural in $T$.
208	\end{theorem}

211	% (proof of lemma-spec, L553-L600, ~50 lines covering the Zariski sheaf property,
212	% the subfunctors F_i associated to an affine cover, and identifying each F_i
213	% with the affine-base case via lemma-spec-base-change and lemma-spec-affine).
214	% Verbatim quote omitted for length; the structural steps are restated below in
215	% project notation.
216	\begin{proof}
217	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_affine_base, thm:relative_spec_base_change}
218	  \leanok
219	  % NOTE (iter-176 review): proof closed kernel-clean in Lean, but discharges
220	  % against the placeholder body ``RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`` and
221	  % ``structureMorphism _𝒜 := 𝟙 X`` (the iter-173 file-skeleton's
222	  % substantive-consequence weakening). The current Lean proof reduces to
223	  % ``inferInstance`` on ``IsAffineHom (𝟙 X)`` via the priority-900
224	  % ``[IsIso f] → IsAffineHom f`` instance. The Zariski-sheaf / open-subfunctor
225	  % glueing argument below MUST be re-derived in Lean once the genuine
226	  % relative-spectrum construction (``Scheme.GlueData`` over ``Spec(𝒜(U))``,
227	  % gated on the quasi-coherence overlay + sheafified-tensor) lands and both
228	  % bodies upgrade. Until then, no consumer file should rely on
229	  % ``RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty`` as a substantive Yoneda-bijection witness.
230	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor $F$ is a Zariski sheaf
231	  because morphisms of schemes and $\mathcal{O}$-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
232	  Choose an affine open cover $X = \bigcup U_i$. The subfunctor
233	  $F_i \subseteq F$ of pairs $(f, \varphi)$ with $f(T) \subseteq U_i$ is representable by
234	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))$ via the affine-base case
235	  (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) together with the
236	  base-change identification $F_i \cong F_{U_i,\,\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}}$
237	  (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_base_change}). Each $F_i \hookrightarrow F$ is an open
238	  subfunctor (pull back $U_i$ along $f$). The $F_i$ jointly cover $F$ since the $U_i$
239	  cover $X$. The gluing of representable open subfunctors produces a representing scheme,
240	  which is canonically isomorphic to the glued scheme $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$
241	  of Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
242	\end{proof}

247	\begin{theorem}\leanok
248	[Relative spectrum over an affine base]
249	  \label{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
250	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff}
251	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
252	  % weaker ``IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜)'' rather than the canonical
253	  % iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))'' pinned by this prose. The Lean name
254	  % ``affine_base_iff'' is also misleading (the encoded type is not an iff).
255	  % Iter-174+ refines to the iff-with-Γ statement once Γ : QcohAlgebra → CommRingCat
256	  % is available.
257	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists}
258	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine)
259	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L491-L545).
260	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
261	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
262	  %  Let $F$ be the functor associated to $(S, \mathcal{A})$ above.
263	  %  If $S$ is affine, then $F$ is representable by the
264	  %  affine scheme $\Spec(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{A}))$."
265	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine).}
266	  Let $X = \Spec R$ be an affine scheme and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent
267	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra. Set $A := \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})$, regarded as an
268	  $R$-algebra. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of $X$-schemes
269	  \[
270	    \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\; \Spec(A).
271	  \]
272	  In particular, on an affine base the relative spectrum reduces to the absolute
273	  spectrum of the global sections.
274	\end{theorem}

291	% \longrightarrow
292	% \widetilde A = \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(A)}
293	% $$
294	% coming from the $A$-module map $A \otimes_R A \to A$,

299	  \leanok
300	  % NOTE (iter-176 review): proof closed kernel-clean in Lean, but discharges
301	  % against the placeholder body ``RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X``. The current Lean
302	  % proof is ``change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`` against the canonical

342	  % lemma-spec-properties part (2))
343	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L467-L489 + L662-L691).

361	  Let $g : T \to X$ be a morphism of schemes and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent

363	  $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebra and there is a canonical isomorphism of $T$-schemes

371	% is the same as a pair $(f, \varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
372	% together with a factorization of $f$ as $f = g \circ f'$. Namely with
373	% this notation we have
374	% $(f')^* \mathcal{A}' = (f')^*g^*\mathcal{A} = f^*\mathcal{A}$.
375	% Hence the lemma."

377	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_univ}
378	  \leanok
379	  % NOTE (iter-176 review): proof closed kernel-clean in Lean, but discharges
380	  % against the placeholder body ``RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`` /
381	  % ``structureMorphism _𝒜 := 𝟙 X``. The current Lean proof witnesses the
382	  % existential with the trivial pullback-algebra carrier and the iso
383	  % ``asIso (pullback.fst g (𝟙 X)) : pullback g (𝟙 X) ≅ T`` via
384	  % ``pullback_fst_iso_of_right_iso`` — it does NOT verify the canonical
385	  % base-change iso of relative spectra named below. The Yoneda argument MUST
386	  % be re-derived in Lean once the genuine relative-spectrum construction
387	  % lands and both bodies upgrade.
388	  By Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}, $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents
389	  the functor $F$ sending an $X$-scheme $h : S \to X$ to the set of
390	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra maps $h^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$. A morphism
391	  $S \to T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ over $T$ is the same as a pair of an
392	  $X$-morphism $S \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with a $T$-factorisation
393	  of $S \to X$, i.e.\ a morphism $S \to T$ whose composition with $g$ equals
394	  $S \to X$. Tracing through, this is a $T$-morphism $f' : S \to T$ together with an
395	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map $(f')^*(g^*\mathcal{A}) = f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$.

397	  $T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \underline{\Spec}_T(g^*\mathcal{A})$

411	  % ``Functor''), with morphism action + functoriality + the equivalence
412	  % onto affine X-schemes all deferred. Iter-174+ upgrades to the full
413	  % contravariant ``Functor (X.QcohAlgebra)ᵒᵖ (Over X)'' with the universal
414	  % property-induced ``map'' once QcohAlgebra is unpacked.
415	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
416	  % lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties part (3))
417	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L602-L691).
418	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
419	  % "Let $S$ be a scheme. Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf of
420	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras. The {\it relative spectrum of $\mathcal{A}$ over
421	  %  $S$}, or simply the {\it spectrum of $\mathcal{A}$ over $S$} is the scheme
422	  %  constructed in Lemma \ref{lemma-glue-relative-spec} which represents the
423	  %  functor $F$ (\ref{equation-spec}), see
424	  %  Lemma \ref{lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec}.
425	  %  We denote it $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \to S$.
426	  %  The ``universal family'' is a morphism of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras
427	  %  $$
428	  %  \mathcal{A}
429	  %  \longrightarrow
430	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
431	  %  $$
432	  %  [...]
433	  %  The universal map
434	  %  $$
435	  %  \mathcal{A}
436	  %  \longrightarrow
437	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
438	  %  $$
439	  %  is an isomorphism of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras."
440	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
441	  lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties);
442	  [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
443	  The construction $\mathcal{A} \mapsto \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ assembles into
444	  a contravariant functor
445	  \[
446	    \underline{\Spec}_X : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AffSch}/X
447	  \]
448	  from quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras to affine $X$-schemes: a morphism of
449	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras $\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ induces, by the universal
450	  property (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) applied to the pair
451	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B}, \psi \circ \pi_\mathcal{B}^*) :
452	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{A} \to \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{B} \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec\mathcal{B}}$,
453	  an $X$-morphism
454	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\psi) : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$.
455	  Functoriality (identity, composition) is direct from the universal property. The
456	  pushforward $\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}$
457	  exhibits $\underline{\Spec}_X$ as an equivalence between $\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}$ and
458	  the full subcategory of affine $X$-schemes, with inverse $f \mapsto f_* \mathcal{O}_Y$
459	  (Stacks lemma-spec-properties part~(3)).
460	\end{theorem}
461	
462	\begin{proof}
463	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ, thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
464	  \leanok
465	  Functoriality follows directly from Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}: for
466	  $\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$, the pair
467	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B} : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to X, \;
468	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*(\psi))$ defines an $X$-morphism
469	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ via the
470	  representing universal pair. Identity and composition follow from naturality of the
471	  representing bijection in $\mathcal{A}$. For the equivalence claim, $\underline{\Spec}_X$
472	  is fully faithful because the isomorphism
473	  $\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}$
474	  (locally identifying with $\Gamma(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)),\mathcal{O}) = \mathcal{A}(U)$
475	  via Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) reconstructs $\mathcal{A}$ from
476	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$. Essential surjectivity onto affine $X$-schemes uses
477	  the canonical morphism $X \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\pi_*\mathcal{O}_X)$ of Stacks
478	  lemma-canonical-morphism; on an affine $X$-scheme $\pi : Y \to X$, this canonical
479	  morphism is an isomorphism (affine-locally it is the identity
480	  $\Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y) \to \Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y)$).
481	\end{proof}
482	
483	\section{Lean encoding}
484	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
485	
486	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
487	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
488	$U \subseteq X$ with $U = \Spec R$ and $A := \mathcal{A}(U)$ regarded as an
489	$R$-algebra, the local piece is $\Spec A$ with structure morphism $\Spec A \to \Spec R = U$
490	induced by the algebra unit $R \to A$. Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
491	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
492	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of $\mathcal{A}$ via
493	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
494	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for $V = \Spec S \subseteq U$).
495	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
496	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
497	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
498	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) is expressed as a
499	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
500	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
501	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
502	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. The base-change isomorphism
503	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_base_change}) and functoriality
504	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_functorial}) are derived from the universal property by
505	Yoneda. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a definitional/structural
506	construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme infrastructure.
507	
508	\section{Out of scope}
509	\label{sec:relspec_out_of_scope}
510	
511	This chapter packages \emph{only} the relative-spectrum construction and its three core
512	properties. The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling chapters and
513	are explicitly out of scope here:
514	\begin{itemize}
515	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$} (A.1.c) --- consumed-by, not
516	    defined-here; its blueprint home is the planned chapter
517	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}.
518	  \item \textbf{Line-bundle pullback on a product $C \times_k T$} (A.1.b) ---
519	    \texttt{Picard\_LineBundlePullback.tex} (planned).
520	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$}
521	    --- downstream properties that depend on assumptions on $\mathcal{A}$
522	    (locally finite type, flat, etc.), not unconditional consequences of the construction.
523	  \item \textbf{Identity-component $\Pic^0$ and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a
524	    separate Route~A sub-row, fed by but not constructed in this chapter.
525	\end{itemize}

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Directed
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.UniversallyInjective
10	
11	/-!
12	# Relative gluing
13	
14	In this file we show a relative gluing lemma (see https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01LH):
15	If `{Uᵢ}` is a locally directed open cover of `S` and we have a compatible family of `Xᵢ` over `Uᵢ`,
16	the `Xᵢ` glue to a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` such that `Xᵢ ≅ f⁻¹ Uᵢ`.
17	-/
18	
19	@[expose] public section
20	
21	universe u
22	
23	open CategoryTheory Limits
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
26	
27	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
28	lemma Scheme.isLocallyDirected_of_equifibered_of_injective {J : Type*} [Category J]
29	    {F G : J ⥤ Scheme.{u}} (s : F ⟶ G) [Quiver.IsThin J] (hs : s.Equifibered)
30	    (H : ∀ {i j} (hij : i ⟶ j), Function.Injective (F.map hij))
31	    [(G ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected] :
32	    (F ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected where
33	  cond {i j k} fi fj xi xj heq := by
34	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj, Scheme.forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, Scheme.forget_map] at heq
35	    obtain ⟨l, fli, flj, x, hi, hj⟩ := (G ⋙ Scheme.forget).exists_map_eq_of_isLocallyDirected fi fj
36	        (s.app i xi) (s.app j xj) <| by
37	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
38	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk]
39	      dsimp at heq
40	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← s.naturality, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, heq,
41	        ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, s.naturality]
42	      simp
43	    use l, fli, flj
44	    let e := (hs fli).isoPullback
45	    obtain ⟨z, h1, h2⟩ := Scheme.Pullback.exists_preimage_pullback xi x hi.symm
46	    refine ⟨e.inv z, ?_, ?_⟩
47	    · simp [← h1, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, e]
48	    · apply H fj
49	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
50	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
51	        Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, show flj ≫ fj = fli ≫ fi by subsingleton]
52	      dsimp at heq
53	      simp [e, Functor.map_comp, ← heq, h1]
54	
55	namespace Scheme.Cover
56	
57	variable {S : Scheme.{u}} (𝒰 : S.OpenCover) [Category 𝒰.I₀] [𝒰.LocallyDirected]
58	
59	/--
60	A relative gluing datum over a locally directed cover `𝒰` of `S` is a scheme `Xᵢ` for every
61	`i : 𝒰.I₀` and natural maps `Xᵢ ⟶ Uᵢ` such that for every `i ⟶ j`, the diagram
62	```
63	Xᵢ --> Uᵢ
64	|      |
65	v      v
66	Xⱼ --> Uⱼ
67	```
68	is a pullback square. We bundle this in the form of a functor and an equifibered natural
69	transformation.
70	The `Xᵢ` then glue to a scheme over `S`
71	(see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued`).
72	-/
73	@[stacks 01LH]
74	structure RelativeGluingData where
75	  /-- The schemes `Xᵢ`. -/
76	  functor : 𝒰.I₀ ⥤ Scheme.{u}
77	  /-- The natural maps `Xᵢ ⟶ Uᵢ`. -/
78	  natTrans : functor ⟶ 𝒰.functorOfLocallyDirected
79	  equifibered : natTrans.Equifibered
80	
81	variable {𝒰} (d : RelativeGluingData 𝒰)
82	
83	namespace RelativeGluingData
84	
85	instance {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : IsOpenImmersion (d.functor.map hij) := by
86	  apply MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (d.equifibered hij).flip
87	  infer_instance
88	
89	instance [Quiver.IsThin 𝒰.I₀] : (d.functor ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected := by
90	  apply isLocallyDirected_of_equifibered_of_injective d.natTrans d.equifibered
91	  intro i j hij
92	  exact (d.functor.map hij).injective
93	
94	variable [Small.{u} 𝒰.I₀] [Quiver.IsThin 𝒰.I₀]
95	
96	/--
97	The glued scheme of a relative gluing datum is the colimit over the `Xᵢ`. For the
98	structure map, see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase` and the isomorphisms
99	with the preimages `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase`.
100	-/
101	@[stacks 01LH]
102	noncomputable abbrev glued : Scheme.{u} :=
103	  colimit d.functor
104	
105	/-- The cover of the glued `Xᵢ` given by the `Xᵢ`. -/
106	@[simps!]
107	noncomputable def cover : OpenCover d.glued :=
108	  Scheme.IsLocallyDirected.openCover _
109	
110	instance : Category d.cover.I₀ :=
111	  inferInstanceAs <| Category 𝒰.I₀
112	
113	/-- The structure map from the colimit of the `Xᵢ` to `S`. -/
114	noncomputable def toBase : d.glued ⟶ S :=
115	  colimit.desc _
116	    { pt := S
117	      ι := d.natTrans ≫ 𝒰.functorOfLocallyDirectedHomBase }
118	
119	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
120	lemma ι_toBase (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
121	    colimit.ι d.functor i ≫ d.toBase = d.natTrans.app i ≫ 𝒰.f i := by
122	  simp [toBase]
123	
124	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
125	instance : d.cover.LocallyDirected where
126	  trans {i j} hij := d.functor.map hij
127	  directed {i j} x := by
128	    let xi := pullback.fst (d.cover.f i) _ x
129	    let xj := pullback.snd (d.cover.f i) _ x
130	    obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, uk, h1, h2⟩ :=
131	        𝒰.exists_of_f_eq_f (d.natTrans.app i xi) (d.natTrans.app j xj) <| by
132	      dsimp [functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, xi, xj]
133	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← ι_toBase, pullback.condition_assoc]
134	      simp
135	    use k, fi, fj
136	    obtain ⟨xk, h1, h2⟩ := exists_preimage_of_isPullback (d.equifibered fj) xj uk <| by
137	      apply (𝒰.f j).injective
138	      dsimp only [functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, functorOfLocallyDirected_map]
139	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
140	      simp [xj, h2]
141	    use xk
142	    apply (pullback.snd (d.cover.f i) _).injective
143	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
144	    simp [h1, xj]
145	
146	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
147	lemma preimage_toBase_eq_range_ι (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
148	    d.toBase ⁻¹' (Set.range <| 𝒰.f i) = Set.range (colimit.ι d.functor i) := by
149	  ext x
150	  refine ⟨fun ⟨ui, h⟩ ↦ ?_, ?_⟩
151	  · obtain ⟨j, xj, rfl⟩ := IsLocallyDirected.ι_jointly_surjective _ x
152	    obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, uk, rfl, h⟩ := 𝒰.exists_of_f_eq_f ui (d.natTrans.app j xj) <| by
153	      simp only [h, functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ι_toBase]
154	    obtain ⟨xk, rfl, h2⟩ := exists_preimage_of_isPullback (d.equifibered fj) xj uk <| by
155	      apply (𝒰.f j).injective
156	      simp only [functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, functorOfLocallyDirected_map]
157	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← ι_toBase, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← h]
158	      simp [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
159	    use d.functor.map fi xk
160	    simp [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
161	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
162	    use d.natTrans.app i y
163	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ι_toBase]
164	    simp
165	
166	lemma toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
167	    d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ (𝒰.f i).opensRange = (colimit.ι d.functor i).opensRange :=
168	  TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inj.mp (preimage_toBase_eq_range_ι d i)
169	
170	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
171	lemma isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
172	    IsPullback (d.natTrans.app i) (colimit.ι d.functor i) (𝒰.f i) d.toBase := by
173	  refine ⟨by simp, ⟨PullbackCone.IsLimit.mk _ ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_⟩⟩
174	  · intro s
175	    apply IsOpenImmersion.lift (colimit.ι d.functor i) s.snd
176	    rw [← preimage_toBase_eq_range_ι]
177	    rintro x ⟨x, rfl⟩
178	    use s.fst x
179	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← s.condition]
180	    simp
181	  · intro s
182	    rw [← cancel_mono (𝒰.f i), Category.assoc, ← ι_toBase, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac_assoc,
183	      s.condition]
184	  · simp
185	  · intro s m h1 h2
186	    simpa [← cancel_mono (colimit.ι d.functor i)]
187	
188	end Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData
189	
190	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RelativeGluing
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.DenseSubsite.InducedTopology
11	
12	/-!
13	
14	# The small affine Zariski site
15	
16	`X.AffineZariskiSite` is the small affine Zariski site of `X`, whose elements are affine open
17	sets of `X`, and whose arrows are basic open sets `D(f) ⟶ U` for any `f : Γ(X, U)`.
18	
19	Every presieve on `U` is then given by a `Set Γ(X, U)` (`presieveOfSections_surjective`), and
20	we endow `X.AffineZariskiSite` with `grothendieckTopology X`, such that `s : Set Γ(X, U)` is
21	a cover if and only if `Ideal.span s = ⊤` (`generate_presieveOfSections_mem_grothendieckTopology`).
22	
23	This is a dense subsite of `X.Opens` (with respect to `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`) via the
24	inclusion functor `toOpensFunctor X`,
25	which gives an equivalence of categories of sheaves (`sheafEquiv`).
26	
27	Note that this differs from the definition on stacks project where the arrows in the small affine
28	Zariski site are arbitrary inclusions.
29	
30	-/
31	
32	@[expose] public section
33	
34	universe u
35	
36	open CategoryTheory Limits
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
43	
44	/--
45	`X.AffineZariskiSite` is the small affine Zariski site of `X`, whose elements are affine open
46	sets of `X`, and whose arrows are basic open sets `D(f) ⟶ U` for any `f : Γ(X, U)`.
47	
48	Note that this differs from the definition on stacks project where the arrows in the small affine
49	Zariski site are arbitrary inclusions.
50	-/
51	def Scheme.AffineZariskiSite (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := { U : X.Opens // IsAffineOpen U }
52	
53	namespace Scheme.AffineZariskiSite
54	
55	/-- The inclusion from `X.AffineZariskiSite` to `X.Opens`. -/
56	abbrev toOpens (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) : X.Opens := U.1
57	
58	instance : Preorder X.AffineZariskiSite where
59	  le U V := ∃ f : Γ(X, V.toOpens), X.basicOpen f = U.toOpens
60	  le_refl U := ⟨1, Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit _ isUnit_one⟩
61	  le_trans := by
62	    rintro ⟨U, hU⟩ ⟨V, hV⟩ ⟨W, hW⟩ ⟨f, rfl⟩ ⟨g, rfl⟩
63	    exact hW.basicOpen_basicOpen_is_basicOpen g f
64	
65	lemma toOpens_mono :
66	    Monotone (toOpens (X := X)) := by
67	  rintro ⟨U, hU⟩ ⟨V, hV⟩ ⟨f, rfl⟩
68	  exact X.basicOpen_le _
69	
70	lemma toOpens_injective : Function.Injective (toOpens (X := X)) := Subtype.val_injective
71	
72	instance : PartialOrder X.AffineZariskiSite where
73	  le_antisymm _ _ hUV hVU := Subtype.ext ((toOpens_mono hUV).antisymm (toOpens_mono hVU))
74	
75	/-- The basic open set of a section, as an element of `AffineZariskiSite`. -/
76	@[simps] def basicOpen (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) (f : Γ(X, U.toOpens)) : X.AffineZariskiSite :=
77	  ⟨X.basicOpen f, U.2.basicOpen f⟩
78	
79	lemma basicOpen_le (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) (f : Γ(X, U.toOpens)) : U.basicOpen f ≤ U :=
80	  ⟨f, rfl⟩
81	
82	variable (X) in
83	/-- The inclusion functor from `X.AffineZariskiSite` to `X.Opens`. -/
84	@[simps! obj]
85	def toOpensFunctor : X.AffineZariskiSite ⥤ X.Opens := toOpens_mono.functor
86	
87	instance : (toOpensFunctor X).Faithful where
88	
89	variable (X) in
90	/-- The forgetful functor from `X.AffineZariskiSite` to `Scheme` is isomorphic to `Spec Γ(X, -)`. -/
91	@[simps! hom_app inv_app]
92	def restrictIsoSpec : toOpensFunctor X ⋙ X.restrictFunctor ⋙ Over.forget _ ≅
93	    toOpensFunctor X ⋙ X.presheaf.rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec :=
94	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun U ↦ U.2.isoSpec)
95	    fun _ ↦ (Scheme.Opens.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_presheaf_map ..).symm
96	
97	section GrothendieckTopology
98	
99	instance : (toOpensFunctor X).IsLocallyFull (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) where
100	  functorPushforward_imageSieve_mem := by
101	    intro U V h x hx
102	    obtain ⟨f, hfU, hxf⟩ := V.2.exists_basicOpen_le ⟨x, hx⟩ (h.le hx)
103	    exact ⟨X.basicOpen f, homOfLE hfU, ⟨V.basicOpen f,
104	      ⟨_, (X.basicOpen_res f h.op).trans (inf_eq_right.mpr hfU)⟩, 𝟙 _,
105	      ⟨⟨f, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, hxf⟩
106	
107	instance : (toOpensFunctor X).IsCoverDense (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) where
108	  is_cover := by
109	    intro U x hx

239	section PreservesLocalization
240	
241	/-!
242	## "Quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras"
243	
244	A presheaf `F` of rings on `X.AffineZariskiSite` with a structural morphism `α : 𝒪ₓ ⟶ F`
245	is said to be `Coequifibered` if `F(D(f)) = F(U)[1/f]`
246	for every open `U` and any section `f : Γ(X, U)`.
247	(See `coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`)
248	
249	Under this condition we can construct a family of gluing data (See `relativeGluingData`) and glue
250	`F` into a scheme over `X` via `(relativeGluingData _).glued`,
251	Also see the relative gluing API in `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean`.
252	
253	This is closely related to the notion of quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras, and we shall link them
254	together once the theory of quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras are developed.
255	-/
256	
257	variable (X) in
258	/-- `X` is the colimit of its affine opens. See `isColimit_cocone` below. -/
259	@[simps] noncomputable def cocone :
260	    Limits.Cocone (toOpensFunctor X ⋙ X.presheaf.rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec) where
261	  pt := X
262	  ι.app U := U.2.fromSpec
263	  ι.naturality {U V} f := by dsimp; rw [V.2.map_fromSpec U.2]; simp
264	
265	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
266	lemma coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway {F : X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat}
267	    {α : (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ F} :
268	    α.Coequifibered ↔ ∀ (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) (f : Γ(X, U.1)),
269	      letI := (F.map (homOfLE (U.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom.toAlgebra
270	      IsLocalization.Away (α.app (.op U) f) (F.obj (.op (U.basicOpen f))) := by
271	  trans ∀ (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) (f : Γ(X, U.1)),
272	    IsPushout (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op)
273	      (α.app _) (α.app (.op (U.basicOpen f))) (F.map (homOfLE (U.basicOpen_le f)).op)
274	  · refine ⟨fun H U f ↦ H (homOfLE (U.basicOpen_le f)).op, fun H ⟨V⟩ ⟨U⟩ ⟨f, hf⟩ ↦ ?_⟩
275	    obtain rfl : V.basicOpen f = U := Subtype.ext hf
276	    exact H V f
277	  refine forall₂_congr fun U f ↦ ?_
278	  set αU : Γ(X, U.toOpens) ⟶ F.obj (.op U) := α.app (.op U)
279	  set αUf : Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) ⟶ F.obj (.op (U.basicOpen f)) := α.app (.op (U.basicOpen f))
280	  algebraize [(X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom, αU.hom, αUf.hom,
281	    (F.map (U.basicOpen_le f).hom.op).hom, (F.map (U.basicOpen_le f).hom.op).hom.comp αU.hom]
282	  have : IsScalarTower Γ(X, U.toOpens) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) (F.obj (.op (U.basicOpen f))) :=
283	    .of_algebraMap_eq' congr($(α.naturality (U.basicOpen_le f).hom.op).hom).symm
284	  have : IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen _
285	  refine (CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout ..).trans ?_
286	  rw [Algebra.IsPushout.comm]
287	  refine (Algebra.isLocalization_iff_isPushout (.powers f) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)).symm.trans ?_
288	  simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
